pages.full_text:
Eve's chocolate paper from lunch.

The campsite is really lovely, perched high on a valley side, so high that planes fly along the valley below us. Although the valley is the same height as where we skied last year ([?Morgie]) the ski hills extend 700m higher.

__Wednesday August 15th.__
We packed up & drove to a super market near St. Jean de Maurienne. We drove north west, to the west of Aix les Bains, north of Chambery, until we reached __St Rampart__ **. It's very hot & we had to wait until the [?accueil] lady came back to get jetons for a shower. Camping Hermitage.
We couldn't find a restaurant open for supper. Simon said the town reminded him of a Black Country town, or Stroud.
Thursday
Rain after much lightening in the night.
Rain, rain, rain in the morning.
pages.notes: A gold-foil and red chocolate wrapper (label reads 'Chocolat noir 72% de cacao / Fine / Marc Christine') is glued in at the top, captioned 'Eve's chocolate paper from lunch.' The place 'St Rampart' is underlined and followed by '**'; could also read '[?St Rambert]' — St-Rambert-en-Bugey or similar. '[?Morgie]' is a tentative reading of the ski resort name from previous year. '[?accueil]' is a tentative reading. Year cannot be confirmed from this page alone; the August 15th Wednesday date fits e.g. 2007 but other years are possible.

pages.full_text:
to the left

[route sketch — see notes]

The gorge widened to become a plain with vinyards on south-facing slopes as we approached Chambery.

ahead to the right

We drove north on the motorway to Dijon, then took the main road. At [?Lenglary], we found a restaurant with just "Menu du jour €13" outside the door. It was delicious, all for £10 each — prosciuto & melon, chicken & potatoes lashed in cream, 2 cheeses, apricot flan, coffee & chocolate & red wine. We bought bread at the bakers & continued on to beyond Troys, Camp Municipal, __Méry-sur-Seine__ **. We've crossed the Rhône-Rhine canal today & the Saûne & the Seine. The weather cheered up after Dijon, but all the rivers & streams look very swollen.
pages.route_sketch_description: A hand-drawn cross-section / profile sketch occupying the top third of the page, showing mountain or valley silhouettes drawn in blue ink. Two annotation labels: 'to the left' (pointing left) and 'ahead to the right' (pointing right/forward). The sketch appears to illustrate the view from the road — mountains/ridges visible to the left and ahead-right as the vehicle travelled through a valley or gorge.
pages.notes: Page number 69 visible in top-right corner. '[?Lenglary]' is uncertain — could be Langres, or a village name; the 'y' ending makes Langres less likely but it is on the route Dijon→Troyes. 'Troys' is the diary's spelling of Troyes. 'Saûne' appears to be the diary's spelling of Saône. 'Méry-sur-Seine' is underlined and followed by '**', indicating overnight stop. The route sketch annotations 'to the left' and 'ahead to the right' describe mountain views seen from the road.
